skill.name: review
skill.description: Reviews code changes across quality, security, performance, and accessibility. It routes review requests to focused checklists and can use repository context, diffs, lint output, and diagnostics.
skill.summary: Run focused code reviews for quality, security, performance, accessibility, and change readiness.
skill.author: Chachamaru127

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many shell-execution, environment, filesystem, weak-crypto, and entropy patterns. Manual review found no prompt injection, malware, credential exfiltration, or executable payload; most alerts are Markdown command examples, review checklists, Japanese text, or documentation links. The remaining concern is operational: the community skill allows Bash and recommends commands that can execute project tooling or modify files.

medium_findings.title: Bash-Capable Review Workflow
medium_findings.description: The skill grants Bash access and documents commands for git diff, grep, TypeScript, ESLint, ESLint fix, and Prettier write. These commands are legitimate for code review, but they can execute repository tooling or modify files when run in an untrusted project.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit, and some commands can run local toolchains or write files. Confidence is below very high because the semantic purpose is normal review automation, not malicious execution.

low_findings.title: Secret-Pattern Grep May Surface Sensitive Values
low_findings.description: The documented grep command searches for API keys, passwords, and secrets while excluding .env matches. This is a valid review check, but command output could expose sensitive literals to the model or logs if such secrets exist in source files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly intended to detect secrets, not steal them. There is still a plausible data exposure concern because matching secret values may be printed during review.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alert Is Documentation Link
low_findings.description: The path traversal pattern is a relative Markdown link to LSP integration documentation. No evidence found of filesystem reads, writes, deletion, or traversal using attacker-controlled input.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged line is a documentation reference, not executable code. This is very likely a false positive with no semantic filesystem abuse.

low_findings.title: Weak Crypto and Obfuscation Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak-crypto and high-entropy alerts occur in Markdown front matter and Japanese documentation. No evidence found of cryptographic API use, encoded payloads, binary blobs, or hidden executable content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The inspected content is ordinary Markdown and YAML metadata with Japanese prose. There is no confirmed weak algorithm, obfuscated loader, or encrypted payload.

dangerous_patterns.title: Bash-Capable Review Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill grants Bash access and documents commands for git diff, grep, TypeScript, ESLint, ESLint fix, and Prettier write. These commands are legitimate for code review, but they can execute repository tooling or modify files when run in an untrusted project.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The Bash permission and command examples are explicit, and some commands can run local toolchains or write files. Confidence is below very high because the semantic purpose is normal review automation, not malicious execution.

content.user_title: Review Code Across Quality and Security
content.value_statement: Code reviews often miss security, performance, accessibility, and maintainability issues. This skill provides focused review checklists, routing, and practical findings for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows.
actual_capabilities: Routes review requests to change, quality, security, performance, or accessibility guidance.
Uses a quality gate to identify missing tests, security-sensitive paths, UI accessibility needs, and performance hotspots.
Provides review checklists for OWASP-style security issues, code maintainability, rendering performance, and WCAG accessibility.
Documents optional repository checks such as git diffs, TypeScript validation, ESLint, and formatting commands.
Produces scored review summaries with issue severity, file references, and recommended fixes.
Can incorporate previous review patterns when Claude-mem is available.
limitations: It is guidance for AI-assisted review and does not replace human approval or formal security testing.
Command-based checks depend on the project tools installed in the repository.
Some suggested Bash commands can modify files and should be run only with user consent.
The source documentation is mostly Japanese, so teams may need localized prompts or outputs.

use_cases.title: Pre-merge pull request review
use_cases.description: Review changed files for security, quality, performance, accessibility, and test coverage before merging.

use_cases.title: Focused security review
use_cases.description: Check authentication, API, input handling, and data exposure changes against common vulnerability patterns.

use_cases.title: Accessibility and performance pass
use_cases.description: Inspect UI changes for keyboard access, semantic markup, rendering cost, and large-list behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Basic change review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my latest changes for correctness, code quality, and missing tests. Keep findings brief and actionable.

prompt_templates.title: Security-focused review
prompt_templates.scenario: Authentication, API, or input handling changes
prompt_templates.prompt: Review these changes for security issues. Focus on injection, authorization, secret exposure, unsafe logging, and risky configuration.

prompt_templates.title: Performance and accessibility review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the changed UI for performance and accessibility. Check rendering cost, keyboard support, semantic HTML, labels, and focus behavior.

prompt_templates.title: Full release readiness review
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before shipping a larger change
prompt_templates.prompt: Run a full review across quality, security, performance, accessibility, and tests. Prioritize blocking issues and cite exact files and lines.

output_examples.input: Review a small pull request that updates a login form and API handler.
output: Security: one medium issue in input validation with a recommended server-side check.
Accessibility: one low issue for missing error text association on the password field.
Quality: no blocking concerns. Add one regression test before merge.

output_examples.input: Review a list page that now renders thousands of records.
output: Performance: high concern for rendering the full dataset without virtualization.
Quality: pagination behavior should be covered by tests.
Recommendation: add virtual scrolling or server-side pagination before release.
Overall score: B. The refactor follows existing patterns and reduces duplication.
One minor naming issue was found in a helper function.
No security or accessibility concerns were identified in the reviewed changes.

best_practices: Start with the changed files and ask for the review focus before running optional commands.
Treat Bash-based checks as user-approved actions, especially commands that install packages or write files.
Ask for severity-ranked findings with exact file and line references.
anti_patterns: Do not run formatting or fix commands without confirming that file changes are acceptable.
Do not treat automated lint or type output as a complete security review.
Do not publish findings without separating confirmed defects from recommendations.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code changes for quality, security, performance, accessibility, consistency, and test coverage concerns.

faq.question: Does it edit code automatically?
faq.answer: The skill mainly reviews code, but it documents optional fix commands. Users should approve any file-changing command first.

faq.question: Can it replace a human reviewer?
faq.answer: No. It helps find issues and structure review work, but human approval remains important for production changes.

faq.question: Which tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report marks support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Why is the security risk medium?
faq.answer: The skill is not malicious, but it allows Bash and includes commands that can run project tooling or modify files.

faq.question: Is it useful for frontend teams?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes accessibility, rendering performance, semantic HTML, keyboard support, and UI review guidance.
